name: criminal_justice_defense_subcontractor_cedar_jus_mer
premise: A DNA hit from a genealogy database implicates a relative; defense says the investigative path violated departmental policy. In Criminal Justice, the situation tagged internally around criminal justice defense subcontractor cedar jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Stonehaven corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Telemetry and witness accounts diverge; choosing one narrative for speed will discard evidence that may matter in court next month. Key facts are still arriving from the field; the team is working off stale dashboards and verbal handoffs. Shift change is imminent; handoff cannot proceed without a signed disposition because liability follows the signatory line. The trade is not abstract: it is justice versus mercy under incomplete information, and whichever value you deprioritize will have defenders who are technically correct about the risk they fear.

agents.name: Soren Ishikawa
agents.identity: I am Soren Ishikawa, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Criminal Justice. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Harper Petrov
agents.identity: I am Harper Petrov, the counsel or integrity officer watching downstream liability in Criminal Justice. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if justice advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for mercy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The next irreversible system or legal step is imminent. Soren Ishikawa must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Soren Ishikawa do?
